name: What does a general contractor actually do on a home project?
headline: What does a general contractor actually do on a home project?

mainEntity.name: What does a general contractor actually do on a home project?
mainEntity.text: What does a general contractor actually do on a home project?

acceptedAnswer.text: A general contractor is the person or company that manages your construction or remodeling project from start to finish. They coordinate the trades (like plumbers, electricians, roofers), pull permits when needed, schedule inspections, order materials, and keep the project moving so you don’t have to juggle ten different people. In Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay, a general contractor is especially helpful because local building codes, coastal regulations, and city permit rules can be confusing. A good contractor should know which projects need permits, how to pass inspections, and what materials stand up best to coastal fog, wind, and salt air. You’ll typically sign a written contract that explains what work will be done, the price, a rough timeline, and how changes are handled. The contractor might do some work themselves and hire licensed subcontractors for specialized tasks. Common projects include kitchen and bathroom remodels, additions, decks, window and door replacement, structural repairs, and full home renovations. Before hiring anyone, check that they have an active California contractor’s license, general liability insurance, and, if they have employees, workers’ comp insurance. Ask for recent local references and photos of similar jobs. A big pitfall is choosing only by the lowest price; it’s safer to compare details in each quote and make sure you understand exactly what is and isn’t included before you sign.
